ontology.foodon_gap_note: No specific FoodOn term exists for affogato. FOODON_03301116 (ice cream food product) is the closest parent category covering the dominant component.
ontology.foodex2_description: Ice cream and similar frozen desserts
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC covers ice cream and similar frozen desserts; used as closest parent as no affogato-specific code exists in the FoodEx2 catalogue.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept found for affogato or espresso-topped ice cream dessert.

serving.description: 1 serving (1 scoop vanilla ice cream ~80g + 1 shot espresso ~30g)
serving.natural_language_portion: A standard affogato serving consists of one scoop of vanilla ice cream (approximately 80 g) topped with one single shot of freshly brewed espresso (approximately 30 g), totalling about 110 g.

nutrient_source.title: Ice Creams, vanilla (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy, FDC ID 167574) and Beverages, coffee, brewed, espresso, restaurant-prepared (FDC ID 171890)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Vanilla affogato is a two-component dish: vanilla ice cream and espresso. Nutrient values are estimated by combining USDA SR Legacy data for vanilla ice cream (FDC ID 167574, ~73% of serving weight) and USDA SR Legacy data for brewed espresso (FDC ID 171890, ~27% of serving weight), then recalculating to a per-100g basis. Weighted blend: 73g ice cream + 27g espresso per 100g combined. This is the best available approved-source estimate in the absence of a direct composite entry.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values 2021 — ice cream, vanilla (GI ~37)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS et al., Diabetes Care 2021
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for vanilla affogato. The GI of 37 is taken from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for vanilla ice cream (full-fat), which is the dominant glycemic component of this dish. Espresso contributes negligible carbohydrate and does not materially alter the GI. GL per serving calculated as: (GI 37 × carbohydrate per serving 21.8g) / 100 = 8.1. Carbohydrate per serving = 19.8 g/100g × 110g serving = 21.78g ≈ 21.8g.

hypertension.guidance: Vanilla affogato is low in sodium (~57 mg per 110g serving) and does not pose a significant hypertensive risk, though individuals on strict sodium-restricted diets should account for the small dairy-derived sodium contribution.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate sugar content and added sugars from vanilla ice cream
type2_diabetes.guidance: Vanilla affogato has a low GI of approximately 37 but contains around 18.9g of sugar per 110g serving, primarily from the ice cream; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should treat this as an occasional dessert and monitor portion size carefully to avoid postprandial glucose excursions.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from full-fat dairy ice cream (~4.4g per 100g)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The saturated fat content of vanilla affogato (~4.8g per 110g serving) is primarily from full-fat dairy ice cream; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit consumption frequency and consider lower-fat ice cream alternatives to reduce saturated fat intake.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Vanilla affogato is a classic Italian dessert-beverage hybrid consisting of one or two scoops of vanilla gelato or vanilla ice cream 'drowned' (the literal meaning of 'affogato' in Italian) with a freshly pulled single or double shot of hot espresso poured directly over the top. The contrast between the cold, creamy, sweet vanilla ice cream and the hot, bitter, intensely aromatic espresso is the defining sensory experience of the dish. As the espresso melts the ice cream, a rich, bittersweet sauce forms at the base of the glass. The texture transitions from firm and creamy to a semi-liquid pool as the dessert is consumed. Affogato originated in northern Italy and is widely served as a post-dinner dessert or afternoon treat. In Singapore, it appears on café and Italian restaurant menus, often at a premium price point. Nutritionally, vanilla affogato is a moderate-energy dessert (~167 kcal per 110g serving) with a low glycemic index (~37) driven by the full-fat dairy ice cream component. It provides meaningful calcium (~97 mg per serving) and vitamin A (~64 mcg RAE per serving) from the dairy base. The espresso contributes negligible macronutrients but adds caffeine (~60–80 mg per shot), which is clinically relevant for individuals sensitive to stimulants, those with cardiac arrhythmias, or pregnant women. The dish is naturally gluten-free and vegetarian but is not suitable for vegans or those with dairy allergies.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a vanilla affogato?
What is the GI of affogato?
What does affogato mean in Italian?
Is affogato a dessert or a drink?

confidence_reason: No direct composite nutrient entry for vanilla affogato exists in any approved primary source. Values are estimated by weighted combination of USDA SR Legacy entries for vanilla ice cream (FDC ID 167574) and brewed espresso (FDC ID 171890) at a 73:27 ratio reflecting a standard 80g ice cream + 30g espresso serving. GI is taken from the University of Sydney GI Database for vanilla ice cream as the dominant glycemic component. Confidence is medium rather than high because the values are estimated from components rather than measured directly on the composite dish.
notes: Food group corrected from input value 'Grains and staples' to 'Desserts', which accurately reflects the nature of vanilla affogato as an ice cream dessert. Per-100g nutrient values are estimated from a weighted blend of USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries: vanilla ice cream (FDC ID 167574) at 73% weight and brewed espresso (FDC ID 171890) at 27% weight, corresponding to a standard 80g + 30g serving. All -1 values in per_100g represent nutrients not separately reported in the USDA SR Legacy source entries used; these are: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g — however, these have been populated with best-estimate values from the USDA SR Legacy source data where available; no field is set to -1 in this record as the USDA SR Legacy entries provide sufficient micronutrient coverage for both components. GI of 37 sourced from University of Sydney GI Database for full-fat vanilla ice cream as the dominant glycemic component; espresso contributes negligible carbohydrate. Halal status assumed as typical for standard vanilla ice cream and espresso without alcohol-based flavourings; restaurant-specific halal certification should be verified. The input food_group 'Grains and staples' was incorrect and has been corrected to 'Desserts'.
